item.title: Cada loco con su tema
item.author: xynova
item.description: To each their own — a lighthearted nod to quirky obsessions.

item.title: Cube-Cows 🐮📈
item.author: xynova
item.description: This week, our cube‑cows are **freaking out about AGI**, while the only one actually up for any work is still the **rubber duck**.

item.title: How meanings sync 🔄
item.author: xynova
item.description:
🔄‣ How meanings sync :

Shared Feelings → Shared Practices → Shared Coordination → Shared Identity ✅

Language is what happens when you zip a whole mental universe into one thin line of text. Someone else unzips it with a different brain and never gets your exact state, only their reconstruction.

Each sentence is basically a meme: a compressed pattern that their mind inflates into a full story, feeling, or frame. Change the brain and the decompression produces a different simulation, even if the words stay the same.

item.title: MEMEs, as virtual agents
item.author: xynova
item.description:
The word meme comes from evolutionary biologist Richard Dawkins in his 1976 book The Selfish Gene. He built it from the Greek mimema (“that which is imitated”) and shortened it to rhyme with “gene” to highlight memes as cultural analogues of genes.

Religions, ideologies, social hierarchies, and shared fictions like money or nation‑states work this way. Once they take root, they guide behavior, attract followers, punish dissent, and defend against criticism.

item.title: Cow Second Post
item.author: xynova
item.description: This is my first post with a main image
